variants.body_md:
## Introduction (ପରିଚୟ)
This lesson explores "The Camel Division Problem," a classic mathematical puzzle from our English textbook. It demonstrates how a clever application of mathematics can solve seemingly impossible problems and make everyone happy.
ଏହି ପାଠଟି "ଓଟ ବିଭାଜନ ସମସ୍ୟା" ବିଷୟରେ, ଯାହା ଆମ ଇଂରାଜୀ ବହିର ଏକ ଗଣିତ ପଜଲ୍ । ଏହା ଦେଖାଏ ଯେ କିପରି ଗଣିତର ଚତୁର ପ୍ରୟୋଗ ଅସମ୍ଭବ ଲାଗୁଥିବା ସମସ୍ୟାକୁ ସମାଧାନ କରିପାରେ ।

## The Problem (ସମସ୍ୟା)
Three brothers faced a difficult situation after their father's death. Their father's will (ଇଚ୍ଛାପତ୍ର) stated that his 35 camels should be divided among them as follows:
ତିନି ଭାଇ ସେମାନଙ୍କ ବାପାଙ୍କ ମୃତ୍ୟୁ ପରେ ଏକ କଷ୍ଟକର ପରିସ୍ଥିତିର ସମ୍ମୁଖୀନ ହେଲେ । ବାପାଙ୍କ ଇଚ୍ଛା ଅନୁଯାୟୀ, ତାଙ୍କର ୩୫ଟି ଓଟକୁ ସେମାନଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ନିମ୍ନଲିଖିତ ଭାବରେ ବଣ୍ଟାଯିବ:
*   Eldest son: $rac{1}{2}$ of the camels
*   Second son: $rac{1}{3}$ of the camels
*   Youngest son: $rac{1}{9}$ of the camels

The challenge was that 35 camels could not be divided exactly by these fractions to yield whole numbers. For example, $rac{1}{2}$ of 35 is $17.5$, which means $17$ and a half camels. This made the division impossible without harming the animals.
ସମସ୍ୟା ଥିଲା ଯେ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟକୁ ଏହି ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶରେ ସଠିକ୍ ଭାବରେ ବାଣ୍ଟି ହେଉନଥିଲା । ଉଦାହରଣ ସ୍ୱରୂପ, ୩୫ର $rac{1}{2}$ ହେଉଛି $17.5$, ଯାହା ଓଟମାନଙ୍କୁ କ୍ଷତି ନ ପହଞ୍ଚାଇ ବାଣ୍ଟିବା ଅସମ୍ଭବ କରିଦେଲା ।

## The Solution (ସମାଧାନ)
A wise young man, a "math-man," offered a clever solution. He added his own camel to the herd (ପଲ) of 35, temporarily increasing the total number of camels from 35 to 36.
ଜଣେ ବୁଦ୍ଧିମାନ ଯୁବକ, ଜଣେ "ଗଣିତଜ୍ଞ", ଏକ ଚତୁର ସମାଧାନ ପ୍ରଦାନ କଲେ । ସେ ନିଜର ଓଟକୁ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟର ପଲରେ ମିଶାଇଲେ, ଯାହାଦ୍ୱାରା ଓଟର ମୋଟ ସଂଖ୍ୟା ଅସ୍ଥାୟୀ ଭାବରେ ୩୫ରୁ ୩୬କୁ ବୃଦ୍ଧି ପାଇଲା ।

Now, with 36 camels, the division became straightforward:
ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ, ୩୬ଟି ଓଟ ସହିତ, ବିଭାଜନ ସହଜ ହୋଇଗଲା:
*   Eldest son: $rac{1}{2} \times 36 = 18$ camels
*   Second son: $rac{1}{3} \times 36 = 12$ camels
*   Youngest son: $rac{1}{9} \times 36 = 4$ camels

All brothers received whole camels, and they were happy because they received slightly more than their original fractional share of 35 camels.
ସମସ୍ତ ଭାଇ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଓଟ ପାଇଲେ, ଏବଂ ସେମାନେ ଖୁସି ଥିଲେ କାରଣ ସେମାନେ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟର ମୂଳ ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶ ଅଂଶଠାରୁ ସାମାନ୍ୟ ଅଧିକ ପାଇଲେ ।

## The Math Behind the Magic (ଏହି ଯାଦୁ ପଛର ଗଣିତ)
After distributing the camels, let's calculate the total number of camels given to the brothers:
ଓଟ ବଣ୍ଟନ କରିବା ପରେ, ଭାଇମାନଙ୍କୁ ଦିଆଯାଇଥିବା ମୋଟ ଓଟ ସଂଖ୍ୟା ଗଣନା କରିବା:
$$18 + 12 + 4 = 34 \text{ camels}$$
The total number of camels available was 36. After giving out 34 camels, there were $36 - 34 = 2$ camels remaining.
ମୋଟ ୩୬ଟି ଓଟ ଉପଲବ୍ଧ ଥିଲା । ୩୪ଟି ଓଟ ଦେବା ପରେ, $୩୬ - ୩୪ = ୨$ଟି ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା ।

The young math-man took back his original camel, and surprisingly, there was still one camel left over! This extra camel became his reward for solving the problem. The "magic" lies in the sum of the fractions:
ଯୁବ ଗଣିତଜ୍ଞ ନିଜର ମୂଳ ଓଟଟିକୁ ଫେରାଇ ନେଲେ, ଏବଂ ଆଶ୍ଚର୍ଯ୍ୟଜନକ ଭାବରେ, ଆଉ ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା! ଏହି ଅତିରିକ୍ତ ଓଟଟି ସମସ୍ୟା ସମାଧାନ ପାଇଁ ତାଙ୍କର ପୁରସ୍କାର ହେଲା । ଏହି "ଯାଦୁ" ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶର ଯୋଗଫଳରେ ନିହିତ:
$$rac{1}{2} + rac{1}{3} + rac{1}{9} = rac{9}{18} + rac{6}{18} + rac{2}{18} = rac{17}{18}$$
Since the sum of the fractions is less than 1 ($rac{17}{18} < 1$), it means a portion of the camels (in this case, $rac{1}{18}$) is always left undistributed. By temporarily increasing the total to 36, this $rac{1}{18}$ portion became $rac{1}{18} \times 36 = 2$ camels. One was the borrowed camel, and the other was the math-man's gain (ଲାଭ).

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Initial Problem: 35 Camels] --> B{Father's Will:};
    B --> B1[Eldest: 1/2];
    B --> B2[Second: 1/3];
    B --> B3[Youngest: 1/9];
    B1 & B2 & B3 --> C{Difficult to Divide 35};
    C --> D[Math-Man's Solution: Add 1 Camel];
    D --> E[Total Camels: 36];
    E --> F[New Division:];
    F --> F1[Eldest: 1/2 * 36 = 18];
    F --> F2[Second: 1/3 * 36 = 12];
    F --> F3[Youngest: 1/9 * 36 = 4];
    F1 & F2 & F3 --> G[Total Distributed: 34];
    G --> H[Camels Remaining: 36 - 34 = 2];
    H --> I[Math-Man Takes His Camel Back];
    H --> J[1 Camel Left Over (Math-Man's Gain)];
    I & J --> K[Everyone Happy!];
```
ଯେହେତୁ ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶର ଯୋଗଫଳ ୧ ରୁ କମ୍ ($rac{17}{18} < 1$), ଏହାର ଅର୍ଥ ହେଉଛି ଓଟମାନଙ୍କର ଏକ ଅଂଶ (ଏହି କ୍ଷେତ୍ରରେ, $rac{1}{18}$) ସର୍ବଦା ଅବଣ୍ଟିତ ରହିଯାଏ । ମୋଟ ସଂଖ୍ୟାକୁ ଅସ୍ଥାୟୀ ଭାବରେ ୩୬କୁ ବୃଦ୍ଧି କରିବା ଦ୍ୱାରା, ଏହି $rac{1}{18}$ ଅଂଶ $rac{1}{18} \times 36 = 2$ ଓଟ ହୋଇଗଲା । ଗୋଟିଏ ଥିଲା ଧାର ନିଆଯାଇଥିବା ଓଟ, ଏବଂ ଅନ୍ୟଟି ଥିଲା ଗଣିତଜ୍ଞଙ୍କ ଲାଭ ।

## Conclusion (ଉପସଂହାର)
This problem beautifully illustrates how creative thinking and a good understanding of mathematics can resolve complex disputes. The young math-man not only solved the brothers' problem but also gained a camel for his wisdom.
ଏହି ସମସ୍ୟାଟି ଦର୍ଶାଏ ଯେ କିପରି ସୃଜନଶୀଳ ଚିନ୍ତାଧାରା ଏବଂ ଗଣିତର ଭଲ ବୁଝାମଣା ଜଟିଳ ବିବାଦର ସମାଧାନ କରିପାରିବ । ଯୁବ ଗଣିତଜ୍ଞ କେବଳ ଭାଇମାନଙ୍କ ସମସ୍ୟା ସମାଧାନ କଲେ ନାହିଁ, ବରଂ ନିଜ ବୁଦ୍ଧି ପାଇଁ ଏକ ଓଟ ମଧ୍ୟ ପାଇଲେ ।

## What's the Story About? (ଗପଟି କାହା ବିଷୟରେ?)
Imagine three brothers who had 35 camels. Their father passed away and left a will (ଇଚ୍ଛାପତ୍ର) saying how to divide the camels. But the way he wanted them divided was very tricky! This story shows how a clever young man used a simple math trick to solve their big problem.
କଳ୍ପନା କରନ୍ତୁ ତିନି ଭାଇଙ୍କ ପାଖରେ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟ ଥିଲା । ସେମାନଙ୍କ ବାପା ମରିଗଲେ ଏବଂ ଓଟଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ କିପରି ବାଣ୍ଟିବେ ସେ ବିଷୟରେ ଏକ ଇଚ୍ଛାପତ୍ର ଛାଡିଗଲେ । କିନ୍ତୁ ସେ ଯେପରି ବାଣ୍ଟିବାକୁ ଚାହିଁଥିଲେ ତାହା ବହୁତ କଷ୍ଟକର ଥିଲା! ଏହି ଗପଟି ଦେଖାଏ ଯେ କିପରି ଜଣେ ଚତୁର ଯୁବକ ଏକ ସରଳ ଗଣିତ କୌଶଳ ବ୍ୟବହାର କରି ସେମାନଙ୍କ ବଡ଼ ସମସ୍ୟାର ସମାଧାନ କଲେ ।

## The Tricky Problem (କଷ୍ଟକର ସମସ୍ୟା)
The father's will said:
ବାପାଙ୍କ ଇଚ୍ଛାପତ୍ର କହିଲା:
*   Eldest son gets $rac{1}{2}$ of the camels.
*   Second son gets $rac{1}{3}$ of the camels.
*   Youngest son gets $rac{1}{9}$ of the camels.

Now, try to divide 35 camels by these fractions.
ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ, ୩୫ଟି ଓଟକୁ ଏହି ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶରେ ବାଣ୍ଟିବାକୁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କରନ୍ତୁ ।
*   $rac{1}{2}$ of 35 is $17.5$. You can't cut a camel in half!
*   $rac{1}{3}$ of 35 is about $11.66$.
*   $rac{1}{9}$ of 35 is about $3.88$.
This was the big problem (ସମସ୍ୟା) – they couldn't divide the camels fairly and in whole numbers.
ଏହା ଥିଲା ବଡ଼ ସମସ୍ୟା – ସେମାନେ ଓଟଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ଠିକ୍ ଭାବରେ ଏବଂ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ସଂଖ୍ୟାରେ ବାଣ୍ଟି ପାରିଲେ ନାହିଁ ।

## The Clever Idea (ଚତୁର ଉପାୟ)
A smart young man came along. He had an idea! He said, "I will lend (ଧାର ଦେବା) you my camel for a while." So, he added his camel to their 35 camels.
ଜଣେ ଚତୁର ଯୁବକ ଆସିଲେ । ତାଙ୍କର ଏକ ଉପାୟ ଥିଲା! ସେ କହିଲେ, "ମୁଁ ମୋର ଓଟକୁ କିଛି ସମୟ ପାଇଁ ତୁମକୁ ଧାର ଦେବି।" ତେଣୁ, ସେ ନିଜ ଓଟକୁ ସେମାନଙ୍କ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟରେ ମିଶାଇଲେ ।
Now, they had $35 + 1 = 36$ camels.
ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ, ସେମାନଙ୍କ ପାଖରେ $୩୫ + ୧ = ୩୬$ଟି ଓଟ ଥିଲା ।

## How it Worked (ଏହା କିପରି କାମ କଲା)
With 36 camels, the division became super easy!
୩୬ଟି ଓଟ ସହିତ, ବିଭାଜନ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ସହଜ ହୋଇଗଲା!
*   Eldest son: $rac{1}{2} \times 36 = 18$ camels. (He was supposed to get $17.5$, so he was happy!)
*   Second son: $rac{1}{3} \times 36 = 12$ camels. (He was supposed to get less than $12$, so he was happy!)
*   Youngest son: $rac{1}{9} \times 36 = 4$ camels. (He was supposed to get less than $4$, so he was happy!)

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[35 Camels] --> B{Add 1 Camel (from Math-Man)};
    B --> C[Total: 36 Camels];
    C --> D[Eldest: 1/2 of 36 = 18];
    C --> E[Second: 1/3 of 36 = 12];
    C --> F[Youngest: 1/9 of 36 = 4];
    D & E & F --> G[Total Distributed: 18+12+4 = 34];
    G --> H[Remaining: 36 - 34 = 2 Camels];
    H --> I[Math-Man Takes His Camel Back];
    H --> J[1 Camel Left Over (Math-Man's Reward)];
```
Everyone got whole camels and was very satisfied (ସନ୍ତୁଷ୍ଟ).

## The Magic Explained (ଯାଦୁର ବ୍ୟାଖ୍ୟା)
After giving out 18, 12, and 4 camels, the brothers received a total of $18 + 12 + 4 = 34$ camels.
୧୮, ୧୨, ଏବଂ ୪ଟି ଓଟ ଦେବା ପରେ, ଭାଇମାନେ ମୋଟ ୩୪ଟି ଓଟ ପାଇଲେ ।
But there were 36 camels to start with (after adding the extra one). So, $36 - 34 = 2$ camels were left.
କିନ୍ତୁ ଆରମ୍ଭରେ ୩୬ଟି ଓଟ ଥିଲା (ଅତିରିକ୍ତ ଓଟ ମିଶାଇବା ପରେ) । ତେଣୁ, $୩୬ - ୩୪ = ୨$ଟି ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା ।
The young man took his camel back, and there was still one camel remaining! This extra camel was his reward for being so smart. The "magic" happens because the fractions $rac{1}{2} + rac{1}{3} + rac{1}{9}$ don't add up to a full 1. They add up to $rac{17}{18}$, leaving $rac{1}{18}$ of the camels untouched.
ଯୁବକ ଜଣକ ନିଜ ଓଟକୁ ଫେରାଇ ନେଲେ, ଏବଂ ଆହୁରି ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା! ଏହି ଅତିରିକ୍ତ ଓଟ ତାଙ୍କର ବୁଦ୍ଧିମତା ପାଇଁ ପୁରସ୍କାର ଥିଲା । "ଯାଦୁ" ଏଥିପାଇଁ ହୁଏ କାରଣ ଭଗ୍ନାଂଶ $rac{1}{2} + rac{1}{3} + rac{1}{9}$ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ୧ ହୁଏ ନାହିଁ । ସେମାନେ $rac{17}{18}$ ହୁଅନ୍ତି, ଯାହା ଓଟମାନଙ୍କର $rac{1}{18}$ ଅଂଶକୁ ଅବଣ୍ଟିତ ରଖେ ।

## Let's Learn About the Camel Problem! (ଚାଲ, ଓଟ ବିଭାଜନ ସମସ୍ୟା ବିଷୟରେ ଶିଖିବା!)
Hello! Today, we're going to read a very interesting story from your English book called "The Camel Division Problem." It's a great example of how a little bit of smart thinking can solve a big problem.
ନମସ୍କାର! ଆଜି ଆମେ ତୁମର ଇଂରାଜୀ ବହିରୁ "ଓଟ ବିଭାଜନ ସମସ୍ୟା" ନାମକ ଏକ ବହୁତ ମଜାଦାର ଗପ ପଢ଼ିବା । ଏହା ଏକ ବଡ଼ ସମସ୍ୟାର ସମାଧାନ ପାଇଁ କିପରି ଟିକିଏ ବୁଦ୍ଧିମାନ ଚିନ୍ତାଧାରା କାମରେ ଆସେ ତାହାର ଏକ ଉତ୍ତମ ଉଦାହରଣ ।

## Understanding the Challenge (ସମସ୍ୟାକୁ ବୁଝିବା)
Let's start by understanding the problem. There were three brothers, and their father had 35 camels. He wanted to divide them like this: the eldest son gets $rac{1}{2}$, the second son gets $rac{1}{3}$, and the youngest son gets $rac{1}{9}$.
ଆସ, ପ୍ରଥମେ ସମସ୍ୟାକୁ ବୁଝିବା । ତିନି ଭାଇ ଥିଲେ, ଏବଂ ସେମାନଙ୍କ ବାପାଙ୍କର ୩୫ଟି ଓଟ ଥିଲା । ସେ ସେମାନଙ୍କୁ ଏହିପରି ବାଣ୍ଟିବାକୁ ଚାହିଁଥିଲେ: ବଡ଼ ପୁଅକୁ $rac{1}{2}$, ଦ୍ୱିତୀୟ ପୁଅକୁ $rac{1}{3}$, ଏବଂ ସାନ ପୁଅକୁ $rac{1}{9}$ ।

*Pause here and ask your child:* "Can you imagine trying to divide 35 camels by half? What would $rac{1}{2}$ of 35 be? Can we really cut a camel in half?"
*ଏଠାରେ ଅଟକି ଯାଆନ୍ତୁ ଏବଂ ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପିଲାକୁ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ:* "ତୁମେ ୩୫ଟି ଓଟକୁ ଅଧା କରିବାକୁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କରିବା କଥା କଳ୍ପନା କରିପାରୁଛ କି? ୩୫ର $rac{1}{2}$ କେତେ ହେବ? ଆମେ ପ୍ରକୃତରେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟକୁ ଅଧା କରିପାରିବା କି?"

The brothers found it impossible because you can't have half a camel! They were quarreling (ଝଗଡ଼ା କରିବା).
ଭାଇମାନେ ଏହାକୁ ଅସମ୍ଭବ ମନେକଲେ କାରଣ ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟକୁ ଅଧା କରି ହେବ ନାହିଁ! ସେମାନେ ଝଗଡ଼ା କରୁଥିଲେ ।

## Finding a Smart Way (ଏକ ଚତୁର ଉପାୟ ଖୋଜିବା)
Then, a clever young man came. He had an idea! He said, "I will add my camel to your herd (ପଲ) for a little while." So, the 35 camels became $35 + 1 = 36$ camels.
ତା'ପରେ, ଜଣେ ଚତୁର ଯୁବକ ଆସିଲେ । ତାଙ୍କର ଏକ ଉପାୟ ଥିଲା! ସେ କହିଲେ, "ମୁଁ ମୋର ଓଟକୁ ତୁମର ପଲରେ କିଛି ସମୟ ପାଇଁ ମିଶାଇ ଦେବି।" ତେଣୁ, ୩୫ଟି ଓଟ $୩୫ + ୧ = ୩୬$ଟି ଓଟ ହୋଇଗଲା ।

*Pause and ask:* "Why do you think adding one camel helped? What's special about the number 36 compared to 35, especially when we talk about dividing by 2, 3, or 9?"
*ଅଟକି ଯାଆନ୍ତୁ ଏବଂ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ:* "ତୁମେ କାହିଁକି ଭାବୁଛ ଯେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟ ଯୋଡ଼ିବା ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କଲା? ୩୫ ତୁଳନାରେ ୩୬ ସଂଖ୍ୟାଟି କାହିଁକି ବିଶେଷ, ବିଶେଷ କରି ଯେତେବେଳେ ଆମେ ୨, ୩, କିମ୍ବା ୯ ଦ୍ୱାରା ଭାଗ କରିବା କଥା କହୁଛୁ?"

Now, the young man told them to divide the 36 camels.
ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ, ଯୁବକ ଜଣକ ସେମାନଙ୍କୁ ୩୬ଟି ଓଟ ବାଣ୍ଟିବାକୁ କହିଲେ ।

## The Amazing Outcome (ଆଶ୍ଚର୍ଯ୍ୟଜନକ ଫଳାଫଳ)
Let's see how they divided the 36 camels:
ଆସ, ଦେଖିବା ସେମାନେ ୩୬ଟି ଓଟକୁ କିପରି ବାଣ୍ଟିଲେ:
*   Eldest son: $rac{1}{2} \times 36 = 18$ camels.
*   Second son: $rac{1}{3} \times 36 = 12$ camels.
*   Youngest son: $rac{1}{9} \times 36 = 4$ camels.

*Pause and ask:* "How many camels did the brothers get in total? What happened to the remaining camels?"
*ଅଟକି ଯାଆନ୍ତୁ ଏବଂ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ:* "ଭାଇମାନେ ମୋଟ କେତୋଟି ଓଟ ପାଇଲେ? ବଳକା ଓଟଗୁଡ଼ିକର କ'ଣ ହେଲା?"

They gave out $18 + 12 + 4 = 34$ camels. Since they started with 36, there were $36 - 34 = 2$ camels left! The young man took his camel back, and there was still one camel left over, which he got as a reward! Everyone was happy. Isn't that clever?
ସେମାନେ $୧୮ + ୧୨ + ୪ = ୩୪$ଟି ଓଟ ବାଣ୍ଟିଲେ । ଯେହେତୁ ସେମାନେ ୩୬ଟିରୁ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲେ, $୩୬ - ୩୪ = ୨$ଟି ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା! ଯୁବକ ଜଣକ ନିଜ ଓଟକୁ ଫେରାଇ ନେଲେ, ଏବଂ ଆହୁରି ଗୋଟିଏ ଓଟ ବଳକା ରହିଲା, ଯାହାକୁ ସେ ପୁରସ୍କାର ଭାବରେ ପାଇଲେ! ସମସ୍ତେ ଖୁସି ଥିଲେ । ଏହା ଚତୁର ନୁହେଁ କି?

questions: Can you imagine trying to divide 35 camels by half? What would $rac{1}{2}$ of 35 be? Can we really cut a camel in half?
Why do you think adding one camel helped? What's special about the number 36 compared to 35, especially when we talk about dividing by 2, 3, or 9?
How many camels did the brothers get in total?
What happened to the remaining camels?
What was the 'magic' behind this solution?
Can you think of another number of camels (instead of 35) where this trick might also work?
tips: Let them struggle with the math for 30 seconds before offering a hint.
Encourage them to explain the 'magic' in their own words.
Relate the problem to sharing things fairly in everyday life.

variants.body_md:
## What gets asked (କେଉଁ ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ପଚରାଯାଏ)
ଏହି ବିଷୟରୁ ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ କେଉଁ ପ୍ରକାରର ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ଆସିପାରେ ତାହା ଦେଖନ୍ତୁ ।
*   **Direct Comprehension:** Questions about the initial number of camels, the father's will (fractions), and the solution (adding a camel). E.g., "How many camels did the brothers initially have?", "What fractions of camels were each son supposed to receive?"
*   **Problem-Solving Steps:** Questions requiring you to outline the steps taken by the math-man to solve the problem. E.g., "Explain the math-man's strategy to divide the camels."
*   **Calculation-based:** Questions asking for the exact number of camels each son received after the solution was applied. E.g., "Calculate the number of camels each son received when 36 camels were divided."
*   **Understanding the 'Magic':** Questions probing the mathematical reason behind the solution, specifically why there were camels left over. E.g., "What is the mathematical reason why the young man gained (ଲାଭ କଲା) a camel?"
*   **Vocabulary/Phrases:** Questions testing understanding of key terms like "will (ଇଚ୍ଛାପତ୍ର)," "quarrelling (ଝଗଡ଼ା କରିବା)," "herd (ପଲ)," "gained (ଲାଭ କଲା)."

## Common mistakes (ସାଧାରଣ ଭୁଲ୍)
ଏହି ବିଷୟରେ ଛାତ୍ରଛାତ୍ରୀମାନେ କରୁଥିବା କିଛି ସାଧାରଣ ଭୁଲ୍ ।
*   Attempting to divide 35 directly by the fractions, leading to non-whole numbers.
*   Forgetting to add the math-man's camel, or not understanding why it was added.
*   Incorrectly calculating the sum of the fractions ($rac{1}{2} + rac{1}{3} + rac{1}{9}$).
*   Not explaining *why* the math-man gained a camel (i.e., the sum of fractions being less than 1).
*   Confusing the initial number of camels (35) with the temporary total (36) during division.

## Scoring hints (ମାର୍କ୍ ପାଇଁ ସୂଚନା)
ଭଲ ମାର୍କ୍ ପାଇବା ପାଇଁ ଏହି ସୂଚନାଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ମନେରଖନ୍ତୁ ।
*   Clearly state the initial problem: 35 camels and the fractional shares ($rac{1}{2}, rac{1}{3}, rac{1}{9}$).
*   Explain the "math-man's" intervention: adding 1 camel to make the total 36.
*   Show the step-by-step calculation for each son's share using 36 camels:
    *   Eldest: $rac{1}{2} \times 36 = 18$
    *   Second: $rac{1}{3} \times 36 = 12$
    *   Youngest: $rac{1}{9} \times 36 = 4$
*   Calculate the total distributed ($18+12+4=34$) and the remaining camels ($36-34=2$).
*   Justify the "magic" by explaining that the sum of the fractions ($rac{1}{2} + rac{1}{3} + rac{1}{9} = rac{17}{18}$) is less than 1, leaving a remainder.
*   Conclude by stating that the math-man took his camel back and gained the extra one.

## Practice prompts (ଅଭ୍ୟାସ ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ)
ଏହି ପ୍ରଶ୍ନଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ବର୍ତ୍ତମାନ ଅଭ୍ୟାସ କରନ୍ତୁ ।
1.  Describe the original problem faced by the three brothers regarding the camel division.
2.  How did the young math-man solve the problem, and what was the final distribution of camels among the brothers?
3.  Explain the mathematical principle that allowed the young man to gain a camel.
